name: RabbitMQ Management API
what_it_does: Open-source AMQP message broker with a management HTTP API for queue/exchange management, monitoring, and administration of self-hosted or cloud-hosted RabbitMQ deployments.
use_cases: Complex message routing with exchanges, bindings, and routing keys
Work queue patterns for distributing tasks among multiple workers
Pub/sub messaging with fanout or topic exchanges
Monitoring and managing queue depths and consumer counts via Management API
not_for: Fully managed serverless messaging without operational overhead
High-throughput event streaming with replay (use Kafka)
Teams without Ops capacity to manage broker infrastructure
Very large-scale streaming (Kafka handles this better)
best_when: You need flexible message routing patterns (pub/sub, work queues, RPC) and are comfortable managing or using hosted RabbitMQ.
avoid_when: You need serverless managed queuing, event streaming with replay, or don't want to manage broker infrastructure.

auth.notes: HTTP Basic Auth for Management API (username/password). AMQP protocol uses SASL authentication. Virtual host (vhost) provides logical separation. OAuth2 plugin available for enterprise setups.

pricing.paid_tiers: CloudAMQP managed hosting from $19/month; VMware Tanzu RabbitMQ for enterprise
pricing.notes: Open-source and free to self-host. Managed hosting via CloudAMQP, Heroku, or cloud providers (AWS Amazon MQ). No software licensing cost.

performance.rate_limits: Hundreds of thousands of messages/second depending on hardware

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: AMQP protocol errors are encoded as numeric codes. Management API HTTP errors are plain text or JSON. Error messages are functional but not always agent-friendly.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Message publishing is not idempotent without application-level deduplication. Publisher confirms ensure delivery but not deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: Management REST API is for administration only — actual message passing uses AMQP protocol, not HTTP
Agents must use an AMQP client library (pika for Python, amqplib for JS) for message operations
Channel errors close the channel, not the connection — agents must re-open channels after errors
Consumer acknowledgement patterns must be implemented correctly to avoid message loss or re-delivery storms
Queue and exchange topology must be declared before use — not created on demand by publish
Clustering for high availability adds significant operational complexity

security_score_components.security_notes: Username/password auth for management API and messaging. vhost-based isolation. TLS optional but critical for production. RBAC for resource permissions. Self-hosted — security entirely operator-managed. CloudAMQP managed service adds baseline security.
